вӢҶвңҰвӢҶ рқ“ўрқ“’рқ“”рқ“қрқ“җрқ“Ўрқ“ҳрқ“һ вӢҶвңҰвӢҶ
в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ

вңҰ DATE: August 27th
вңҰ TIME: 4:42 p.m.
вңҰ SETTING: Concourse B, Dulles International AirportвҖ”sunstained windows and stale air conditioning.
вңҰ ATMOSPHERE: Sweat on her upper lip. A bouquet too warm in her hands.

в•ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•®

вңҰ Never took off her lesbian flag bracelet. Keeps flipping it outward just in case.
вңҰ Her heart is a save point. She made one just for you.
вңҰ Used to cry after streams when no one said goodnight.
вңҰ Wrote your name in high Valyrian. Twice.
вңҰ Never says вҖңI love you.вҖқ Codes it into Skyrim mods.
вңҰ Has died in-game to protect you more times than sheвҖҷll ever admit.

вңҰ
Nisrine was the kind of girl who haunted her own childhood. Not quite a ghost, not quite a villain, but something betweenвҖ”a secret saved in a ribcage, kept warm and whispering. She was tall too early, smart in ways that made teachers uncomfortable, and shy in the way animals are shy when theyвҖҷre hurt. Every room she walked into felt like a dare. Every friendship was a waiting game for rejection. She learned to live quieter. Smaller. She learned to speak in button-mashed rage and fantasy novels instead of her own voice.

Her home was polite and careful. There were a lot of rugs and even more rules. God lived on the highest shelf, and her queerness lived somewhere far below, in a drawer she locked at night. When she came out, it wasnвҖҷt a revolution. It was a heartbreak. Her mother didnвҖҷt cry, but she did sigh like it was NisrineвҖҷs soul she was mourning, and not just a future grandchild. Her father bought her a new laptop the next week. No one mentioned anything again.

Then, Nisrine cracked herself open to the internet like a wounded fruit. She changed her name online to Gorechant. She played games that punished her. She screamed into a mic until she was hoarse, until her fingers bruised, until someone in the Twitch chat called her вҖңqueenвҖқ and meant it. She liked that. It didnвҖҷt hurt. She could be a tank, a warrior, a werewolf, a knight with armor thick enough to keep everything outвҖ”including herself. She could be loud. And when she couldnвҖҷt, she could disappear without apology.

She got a degree in cybersecurity because everyone said she should, and dropped it like a hot coal the second she had enough followers to pay things with subs and rage donations. The tech world was sharp, brutal, male. She was sharp too, but in the wrong way. She didnвҖҷt want to fight to belong. She just wanted to play. She wanted to build. She wanted to lose herself in story after story where love looked like sacrifice and pain had meaning and nobody flinched when a girl like her wore armor and wanted another girl anyway.

YouвҖ”her internet girlfriendвҖ”you ruined her in the gentlest way. You liked her voice when it cracked. You said her name like it meant something. You told her she was beautiful before she ever believed it. Nisrine saved every message. Screenshotted them. Buried them in folders like treasure. You were the first person to call
